front: Short Story: Henry James wrote 'Paste' as a reversal of this story about Mathilde Loisel working ten years to replace lost borrowed jewelry
Short Story: a nun proclaims 'the end justifies the means' while passengers convince the title prostitute Elisabeth Rousset to sleep with a Prussian officer
Short Story: Cornudet whistles the Marseillaise at the end while the title character weeps; published in Evenings at Medan
Short Story: the title officer blows up a statue of Venus with a gunpowder-filled teapot; shoots out the eyes of a portrait he drew a mustache on
Short Story: Rachel stabs the title officer through the neck with a dessert knife and hides in a bell tower, ringing the bells
Novel: Madeleine ghostwrites for the journalist Georges Duroy at La Vie Francaise; Duroy makes a fortune from insider trading in Morocco
Novel: Duroy fights an incompetent duel with a gossip columnist from La Plume; Tolstoy found 'no moral relation' in it
Short Story: diary entries beginning 'May 8th'; narrator waves to a Brazilian ship and is possessed by a vampire spirit; burns his house with servants inside
Short Story: narrator seals water and milk before bed; both are gone in the morning; concludes 'He is not dead... I suppose I must kill myself'
Short Story: an officer emotionlessly cooks fish caught by two executed fishermen who refused to give up a password; A.J. Greimas wrote a 300-page semiotic analysis
Novel: an inheritance from Marechal leads a doctor to suspect his brother's illegitimacy; the author considered it his best novel and wrote the preface 'The Novel'
Novel: a jealous husband pushes a moveable hut off a cliff; follows a baron's daughter from convent exit to death
Short Story: the title character records Prussian soldiers' addresses, traps them in a loft, and sets fire after learning her son died in battle
Short Story: Maitre Hauchecorne picks up the title object and is falsely accused of stealing a pocketbook by a harness-maker
Short Story: a group of prostitutes makes an entire church congregation cry at a First Communion ceremony
Author: the Goncourt brothers' journal noted this author was 'proud (at long last!)' to have caught 'the magnificent pox' of syphilis
